sourceNote: Authored from POH/AFM PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.10.4 (Engine Flameout). The POH treats Engine Flameout as a short diagnostic/routing procedure that delegates to either 'Engine Failure During Flight' (POH 3.3.4, already implemented as engine_failure_during_flight.json) or 'Normal Airstart' (POH 3.3.5) depending on whether the flameout cause is correctable. Correctable causes cited by the POH: improper fuel management, or failure to extend intake deflectors in visible moisture at temperatures of +5°C or less. This procedure ONLY covers the identification-and-route phase. Once routed, the crew runs the appropriate downstream procedure. FIRST-PASS DRAFT — needs SME review.

memory.action: ENGINE FLAMEOUT L/R — sudden loss of thrust, engine instruments similar to normal shutdown
CONFIRMED L/R FLAMEOUT — I have control, maintaining flight path, asymmetric thrust identified
POWER (UNAFFECTED ENGINE) — SET as required to arrest yaw and hold pitch

flow.action: ENGINE FLAMEOUT L/R — thrust loss confirmed, engine gauges show flame-out signature (Ng decay, T5 falling, torque zero, oil pressure falling toward idle)
CONFIRMED — I have control, maintaining flight path, aircraft trimmed for asymmetric thrust
IDENTIFY CAUSE — was flameout preceded by [FUEL MANAGEMENT ERROR / INTAKE DEFLECTORS NOT EXTENDED IN VISIBLE MOISTURE ≤+5°C / OTHER CAUSE]?
CAUSE REPORTED — [fuel management corrected / intake deflectors now extended / cause unknown or not correctable]
DECISION — IF CAUSE CORRECTABLE: airstart per POH 3.3.5. IF CAUSE NOT CORRECTABLE OR UNKNOWN: shut down per POH 3.3.4.
DECISION REPORTED — proceeding with [NORMAL AIRSTART / ENGINE SHUTDOWN IN FLIGHT / ENGINE FAILURE DURING FLIGHT]
IF ATTEMPTING AIRSTART — run NORMAL AIRSTART checklist per POH 3.3.5. If unsuccessful, revert to ENGINE FAILURE DURING FLIGHT (POH 3.3.4).
IF NOT ATTEMPTING AIRSTART — run ENGINE FAILURE DURING FLIGHT checklist per POH 3.3.4 (feather, secure, cool, monitor).
ATC ADVISED — [CALLSIGN] declaring PAN PAN (engine flameout, attempting airstart) or MAYDAY (unable to restart), request routing/priority
ENGINE FLAMEOUT CHECKLIST COMPLETE — transitioning to downstream checklist ({airstart or engine failure during flight})

memory.action: ENGINE FLAMEOUT L/R — sudden loss of thrust, EIS engine indications show shutdown signature
CONFIRMED L/R FLAMEOUT — I have control, maintaining flight path, asymmetric thrust identified
POWER (UNAFFECTED ENGINE) — SET as required to arrest yaw and hold pitch

flow.action: ENGINE FLAMEOUT L/R — thrust loss confirmed, EIS engine gauges show flame-out signature (Ng decay, T5 falling, torque zero, oil pressure falling toward idle)
CONFIRMED — I have control, maintaining flight path, aircraft trimmed for asymmetric thrust
IDENTIFY CAUSE — was flameout preceded by [FUEL MANAGEMENT ERROR / INTAKE DEFLECTORS NOT EXTENDED IN VISIBLE MOISTURE ≤+5°C / OTHER CAUSE]?
CAUSE REPORTED — [fuel management corrected / intake deflectors now extended / cause unknown or not correctable]
DECISION — IF CAUSE CORRECTABLE: airstart per POH 3.3.5. IF CAUSE NOT CORRECTABLE OR UNKNOWN: shut down per POH 3.3.4.
DECISION REPORTED — proceeding with [NORMAL AIRSTART / ENGINE SHUTDOWN IN FLIGHT / ENGINE FAILURE DURING FLIGHT]
IF ATTEMPTING AIRSTART — run NORMAL AIRSTART checklist per POH 3.3.5. G950 EIS airstart indications confirmed. If unsuccessful, revert to ENGINE FAILURE DURING FLIGHT (POH 3.3.4).
IF NOT ATTEMPTING AIRSTART — run ENGINE FAILURE DURING FLIGHT checklist per POH 3.3.4 (feather, secure, cool, monitor).
ATC ADVISED — [CALLSIGN] declaring PAN PAN (engine flameout, attempting airstart) or MAYDAY (unable to restart), request routing/priority
ENGINE FLAMEOUT CHECKLIST COMPLETE — transitioning to downstream checklist ({airstart or engine failure during flight})

sequenceBasis: POH PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.10.4 authoring pass
